feelike — Tag Suggestions UX Comparison

Comparing two patterns for immediate tag suggestions when a user taps "+ add" on the capture screen. Tokens from ux-spec.md. Light mode.

Option A: Bottom Sheet Option B: Inline Expansion Light mode 375 × 812
Option A Bottom Sheet Initial open — all past tags visible
9:41
feelike
went for a morning walk through the park — forgot how much I needed that
Mood
😊
😔
😤
🤩
😌
😰
Tags
#work ×
#ideas ×
add
Save entry
Tags
Search or create tag…
✓#work
✓#ideas
#personal
#grateful
#health
#travel

Behavior notes

  • Sheet slides up from bottom over 220ms (ease-standard) on "+ add" tap.
  • All past tags shown immediately — zero-char state is not empty.
  • Already-selected tags dimmed to 45% opacity with a checkmark — visible but clearly off-limits. Not hidden (lets user confirm what's already picked).
  • Search auto-focused — keyboard opens, user can type straight away.
  • Drag handle communicates dismissibility (swipe down or tap overlay).
Option A Bottom Sheet Typed "meet" — no existing match → create CTA
9:41
feelike
went for a morning walk through the park — forgot how much I needed that
Mood
😊
😔
😤
🤩
😌
😰
Tags
#work ×
#ideas ×
add
Save entry
Tags
meet
#meet
create new tag
No existing tags match "meet"

Behavior notes

  • Create row surfaces at top when no existing tags match — it becomes the only actionable item, so it claims the primary position.
  • Pill shows #meet in tag-pink so the user sees exactly what will be created before confirming.
  • No matches text explains why the grid is empty — removes confusion vs. silent blank space.
  • On create tap: sheet closes, new tag chip appears in the entry form, #meet added to user's tag library.
Option B Inline Expansion Accordion open · typed "meet" · Save still visible
9:41
feelike
went for a morning walk through the park — forgot how much I needed that
Mood
😊
😔
😤
🤩
😌
😰
Tags
#work ×
#ideas ×
Add tag
meet
#meet
create new tag
✓#work
✓#ideas
#personal
#grateful
#health
#travel
Save entry

Behavior notes

  • No overlay — form context stays visible; user can see the entry text and mood while tagging.
  • Save button remains reachable — user can skip tagging without a dismiss gesture.
  • Accordion animates open at 220ms (ease-standard); closing via × button or tapping outside the section.
  • All past tags still visible even when "meet" is typed — grid is not filtered out, because the create row is the primary action but familiar tags should remain scannable.
  • Note: if the user has many tags (20+) this inline grid becomes a scrolling list capped at ~120px height.

Pattern tradeoffs

Dimension Option A — Bottom Sheet Option B — Inline Expansion
Tag grid real estate Large — sheet uses full width, comfortable tag targets Compact — bounded by remaining form height
Save button reachability Hidden behind sheet — must dismiss first to save Always visible — no extra dismiss gesture needed
Entry text visible during tagging Obscured by overlay + sheet Fully visible — user can re-read entry while picking tags
Keyboard interaction Sheet sits above keyboard area — standard iOS/Android pattern; keyboard pushes sheet up naturally Keyboard pushes the whole form up; save button may scroll off on small phones
Gesture to dismiss Swipe down or tap overlay — familiar bottom-sheet convention Tap × or tap outside section — less standardized
Design principle alignment Capture first — dedicated surface for tags feels intentional, not forced.
One extra dismiss gesture vs. inline. 		Every tap is a cost — no extra dismiss needed.
Crowded on small screens with 10+ tags.
Scalability (many tags) Sheet scrolls gracefully — grid expands within sheet height, user can scroll Inline section caps at fixed height — requires internal scroll, which competes with form scroll
Platform precedent iOS Share Sheet, Apple Maps suggestions, Instagram location picker Twitter "Add Location" accordion, inline autocomplete in form fields
Recommendation Option A (Bottom Sheet) is preferred for feelike v1. The tag grid benefits from the larger surface, the keyboard-above-sheet layout is a well-worn mobile pattern, and the dismiss gesture (swipe down) is universally understood. The one cost — hiding the Save button — is acceptable because the bottom sheet is a transient state with a clear exit path. If the user has 0 past tags, the sheet shows only the empty-search-then-create flow, which is equally clear.
